directions.name: Restore The Best Measured PTX Grouping Window On The Accepted Surface
directions.hypothesis: Round 13 gave a strong negative result on the repaired PTX surface: swapping the future-tile refill from `B->A` to `A->B` regressed runtime from the round-12 best 24.16427231 ms to 24.48483181 ms, even though the dominant hot-band kernel stayed the same PTX microkernel. A direct source diff versus the current best commit `489574e` shows the semantic delta is only that one refill-order hunk at `src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:2042-2053`. The counters also moved in the wrong direction for this family: long-scoreboard dropped sharply, but barrier jumped from 5.43% to 6.29% and total runtime got much worse. The immediate next move is therefore not another exploit on top of the bad state; it is to restore the current best PTX surface first so the loop can continue exploring from the real winner instead of compounding a known negative handoff order.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Known-negative handoff ordering on the active PTX hot-band loop, where the A-then-B future refill increases effective barrier cost enough to overwhelm any scoreboard reduction.
directions.risk: Low. The restore is narrowly bounded and the current source differs from the best-known PTX surface by essentially one scheduler hunk, so this is the cleanest way to recover a valid search anchor after the round-13 regression.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime versus the 24.164272 ms best-known PTX run
Kernel Name in ncu_details.csv for the dominant hot-band launch
ranking_notes: Round 13 is a clean falsification of the A-then-B handoff on the correct PTX surface, not a noisy flat result.
The source diff to the current best run is one bounded hunk, so restoring the best surface is cheaper and more defensible than layering another family onto a known-regressed state.
It ranks first because the loop should recover a valid search anchor before evaluating any other open family.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if restoring the best-known PTX handoff order fails to recover near the 24.164272 ms band, because that would imply the current state drift is wider than the visible one-hunk delta.

directions.name: Continue The Active PTX One-K 128x128 Control-Path Exploit
directions.hypothesis: Once the loop is restored to the round-12 best PTX surface, the broad active-PTX control family remains the strongest open follow-on in the frontier. Round 13 did not invalidate this family; it invalidated one specific handoff sub-variant. The PTX branch still sits at only 16.63% active warps on the best run, and the control family still offers bounded room to reshape issue/consume behavior without abandoning the winning dispatch. It stays second because the immediate task is to restore the search anchor before attempting another exploit.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Residual PTX hot-band control-path overhead and consume-order friction on the restored one-K 128x128 PTX surface, beyond the already-closed A-then-B handoff variant.
directions.risk: Moderate. The family still targets the real hot path, but previous sub-variants have mixed evidence, so another attempt must be materially different from both the B-first prologue and the now-rejected A-then-B handoff.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime versus the 24.164272 ms best-known PTX run
ranking_notes: This is still the top open frontier family after removing the round-13 handoff sub-variant from consideration.
It stays below dir_01 because exploiting on top of a known-regressed state would contaminate the comparison; the best surface should be restored first.
Keeping it second preserves the most coherent active-PTX next move once the restore lands.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the next control-path tweak replays the already-rejected A-then-B handoff or any other previously measured no-op sub-variant.

directions.name: Restore accepted grouped_rows=8 hot-band consumer ordering
directions.hypothesis: The loop should still keep one alternate PTX surface from `round_history` alive in case the grouped_rows=4 branch saturates again after the restore. The grouped_rows=8 consumer-ordering family remains the strongest such fallback in the frontier. It is still a genuine current-source delta and a real alternate PTX locality regime, but it now ranks below both the restore and the active control family because round 12 proved the grouped_rows=4 PTX surface is still the best measured branch when its handoff order is correct.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Consumer-side ordering and grouped-row locality inside the PTX hot-band microkernel under the grouped_rows=8 regime.
directions.risk: Moderate. This is an alternate branch reset onto a historically slower PTX surface, so it should only be used after the loop has recovered the current best grouped_rows=4 anchor.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime versus the 24.164272 ms best-known PTX run
correctness pass rate across all 3 cases
ranking_notes: This stays in the top three because the user explicitly asked to keep promising round-history states alive in the live queue.
It ranks behind the restore and active control family because the current grouped_rows=4 PTX branch remains the overall winner when not distorted by the bad handoff order.
Keeping it third preserves one real alternate PTX locality branch without derailing the immediate recovery step.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the grouped_rows=8 restore remains materially behind the recovered grouped_rows=4 PTX surface without showing a clearly better scheduler signature.

notes: Round 14/100 audit: round 13 cleanly falsified the A-then-B PTX handoff retime on the correct PTX surface. The dominant kernel stayed `bf16_gemm_v1_tensor_core_fixed_hot_band_128x128_ptx_microkernel<(int)452>`, but runtime regressed by 0.320559 ms and barrier rose from 5.43% to 6.29%. The current source differs from the round-12 best PTX surface by essentially one refill-order hunk, so the next action should be to restore that best surface first, then continue active-PTX exploration from a valid anchor while keeping the grouped_rows=8 round-history fallback alive.
